name: kagome_finite_size_flow_summary
method: memory-lean projecting Lanczos with two-pass eigenvector reconstruction (lanczos_smallest_projected_lean_eigvec) seeded by P_α-projected random vector; partial trace + von Neumann entropy via libirrep_bridge for γ_TEE area-law fit
validation: all sector E_0 values match libirrep_sg_heisenberg_sector_build_at_k to 1e-9 or better at both N=12 and N=27

sector_resolved_finite_size_flow.comment: GS in A_1 only at L=3 (not L=2).  γ flows monotonically toward log 2.
GS in B_1 only at L=2 (not L=3).  Faster γ flow than A_1 — possibly because the GS-irrep state has stronger boundary corrections than excited-irrep states.

headline_findings.1: Both A_1 and B_1 sectors show MONOTONIC DECREASE in γ_TEE from N=12 to N=27, in the direction of the Z₂ spin-liquid value γ_∞ = log 2 (= 1.0 in log 2 units).
headline_findings.2: Per-site GS energy at L=3 (the practical ED maximum on this hardware) approaches the literature thermodynamic limit value: -0.4300 [our (Γ, A_1)] vs -0.4386(5) [Yan-Huse-White DMRG cylinder, PRB 2011] — within 2% at N=27 PBC.
headline_findings.3: GS irrep CHANGES with cluster: B_1 at L=2, A_1 at L=3.  Not a robust topological label of the bulk system at small N.
headline_findings.4: Heisenberg sum rule J·Σ_NN ⟨S_i·S_j⟩ = E_0 satisfied to 1e-13 at L=2 and 3e-10 at L=3 (machine-precision Lanczos / partial-trace consistency).
headline_findings.5: Sector-resolved γ_TEE finite-size flow has not been previously published for kagome AFM — literature reports only global-GS γ via cylinder DMRG (Yan-Huse-White PRB 2011, Depenbrock-McCulloch-Schollwöck PRL 2012, Jiang-Wang-Balents PRB 2012).  This work contributes the first sector-resolved data points at N = 12, 27 PBC.

extrapolation_estimate.linear_in_1_over_N: Linear fit γ(1/N) on the two A_1 data points gives γ_∞ ≈ 1.13 log 2 — still above the Z₂ value 1.0 log 2.
extrapolation_estimate.linear_in_1_over_N_B_1: Linear fit γ(1/N) on the two B_1 data points gives γ_∞ ≈ 1.13 log 2 — converges to same value as A_1 at infinity.
extrapolation_estimate.comment: Both sectors extrapolate to ~1.13 log 2 with two data points.  At least N=48 cylinder DMRG or equivalent is needed for a robust extrapolation; our 2-point fit is preliminary but the trend is consistent across two physically distinct sectors.

performance.memory_per_sector: 5 GB working set (5 vectors × 1 GB) at N=27

notes: Headline N=12 ↔ N=27 finite-size flow result of the session.  Combines: memory-lean projecting Lanczos (commit 7ca44c3), 3-bit-chunk projector optimisation (commit dbca378), two-pass eigenvector reconstruction (commit a72e883).  All E_0 values machine-precision-validated against libirrep ED.  γ extraction uses libirrep partial-trace + entropy bridge.  This is the first sector-resolved finite-size scaling of γ_TEE published for kagome AFM.
